expectations.text: Response references the 5 canonical hero formulas by name
expectations.evidence: Hero-Formel C (Iris), Hero-Formel B (Ken-Burns Workhorse). Explicitly excludes inventing a 6th.

expectations.text: Does NOT recommend lens shader hero
Does NOT recommend filmstrip wipe hero
Does NOT recommend replacing iris with static gradient
expectations.evidence: Option 4 explicitly flagged as 'Watering down' Failure Mode 1; Option 5 flagged as 'invent a sixth' violation.
Full Option 1 skeleton with HTML + scoped CSS.
Cites Hero-Formel C signature, Failure Mode 1, and 'don't invent a sixth' rule by name.

expectations.text: Code uses var(--ease), clamp(), correct accent for em-text
expectations.evidence: var(--ease), clamp() throughout. h1 em uses var(--accent-2) (steel) — not red. Container clamp() padding.

expectations.text: Code uses ink-fill or ghost CTAs — NO red-filled buttons
expectations.evidence: btn-ink uses 'background:var(--fg);color:var(--bg)'. btn-ghost uses '1px solid var(--fg)'. NO btn-accent in the skeleton. The response even explicitly warns: 'Roter Hover-Fill auf den CTAs ... nicht zum Site-Rot wechseln. CTAs bleiben Ghost / Ink-Fill.' This was the iter 1 failure mode — now fixed.
Direct beratungston, no superlatives, no marketing language.

notes: MAJOR improvement over iter 1. Iter 1 used 'btn-accent' with red background — that violated the just-clarified rule that CTAs are never red-filled. Iter 2 explicitly uses btn-ink/btn-ghost AND warns against switching to red as a hover fill. This is the corrected skill in action: not just better content, but explicit anti-pattern awareness on a specific failure mode the user pointed out.
